docs.title: AssetFlow
docs.text:
A macOS app for snapshot-based portfolio management and asset allocation tracking.

AssetFlow helps you take control of your investments by recording portfolio snapshots over time. Instead of syncing with brokerage accounts, you capture the state of your portfolio whenever you choose — giving you a clear, private picture of how your assets are allocated and how they grow. Everything stays on your Mac, with no cloud accounts required.

docs.title: Feature Highlights
docs.text:
• Snapshot-Based Tracking

    Record your portfolio's state at any point in time. Compare snapshots to see how your holdings change across weeks, months, or years.

  • Multi-Currency Support

    Track assets in multiple currencies with automatic exchange rate fetching. All values convert to your base currency for a unified view.

  • Category & Target Allocation

    Organize assets into categories like Equities, Bonds, and Cash. Set target allocation percentages to define your ideal portfolio.

  • Rebalancing Calculator

    See exactly what to buy or sell to reach your target allocation. Take the guesswork out of portfolio rebalancing.

  • CSV Import

    Bulk import assets and cash flows from spreadsheets. Get up and running quickly with data you already have.

  • Performance Metrics

    Track growth rate, Modified Dietz return, time-weighted return (TWR), and compound annual growth rate (CAGR).

  • Charts & Visualizations

    Interactive pie charts for allocation breakdowns and line charts for portfolio value over time.

  • Security

    Optional app lock with Touch ID, Apple Watch, or password. Your financial data stays private.

docs.title: What is a "snapshot"?
docs.text: A snapshot is a point-in-time record of your entire portfolio. Think of it like a photograph of your finances on a specific date. Each snapshot stores the market value of every asset you own at that moment.

docs.title: How often should I create snapshots?
docs.text: Monthly is a good starting point for most users. Some prefer weekly or even daily. The more frequently you create snapshots, the more detailed your historical data and performance charts will be.

docs.title: What's the difference between Growth Rate and Return Rate?
docs.text: Growth Rate is the simple percentage change in total portfolio value, including the effect of deposits and withdrawals. Return Rate uses the Modified Dietz method to separate actual investment performance from cash flows, giving you a more accurate picture of how your investments are doing. See Performance Metrics for a detailed explanation.

docs.title: Where is my data stored?
docs.text: All data is stored locally on your Mac using Apple's SwiftData framework. No data is sent to external servers. The only network activity is fetching exchange rates from cdn.jsdelivr.net.

docs.title: Can I use AssetFlow offline?
docs.text: Yes. The only feature that requires internet is exchange rate fetching. Everything else works fully offline.

docs.title: How do I track assets in different currencies?
docs.text: Set each asset's currency in its detail view. AssetFlow automatically fetches exchange rates and converts values to your main currency for display. See Currencies & Exchange Rates for details.

docs.title: Can I import data from my brokerage?
docs.text: If your brokerage exports CSV files, yes. Export your holdings as CSV, make sure it has "Asset Name" and "Market Value" columns, and use the Import CSV tool. See CSV Format for the expected format.

docs.title: What happens if I delete a snapshot?
docs.text: The snapshot and all its recorded values and cash flows are permanently deleted. The assets themselves remain — they just won't have values for that date anymore.

docs.title: Why can't I delete an asset?
docs.text: Assets can only be deleted when they have no values in any snapshot. First remove the asset from all snapshots, then you can delete it.

docs.title: Is my data backed up?
docs.text: Not automatically. Open Settings (Cmd+,) and use Data Management > Export Backup to create a backup ZIP file. See Backup & Restore for details.

docs.title: Troubleshooting
docs.text: Solutions to common issues you might encounter while using AssetFlow.

docs.title: Exchange rates not loading
docs.text:
• Check your internet connection.
  • Try the Retry button in the snapshot detail view.
  • Exchange rates are fetched from cdn.jsdelivr.net — check if this domain is accessible from your network.
  • If you're behind a firewall or VPN, it may block the connection.

docs.title: Exchange rates showing as stale or incorrect
docs.text:
• Rates are fetched for the specific snapshot date. Historical rates reflect the actual rate on that date, not today's rate.
  • If a rate seems wrong, it may be because the data source uses end-of-day rates, which can differ from intraday rates you see elsewhere.

docs.title: Import button is disabled
docs.text: There are validation errors in the preview. Look for red error markers and fix the issues — common causes include missing columns, duplicate assets, or unparsable values.

docs.title: Characters appear garbled after import
docs.text: Your CSV file may not be saved as UTF-8. Re-export from your spreadsheet application with UTF-8 encoding.

docs.title: "Duplicate asset" error
docs.text: An asset with the same name and platform already exists in the target snapshot. Either rename the asset, change its platform, or remove the duplicate row from the CSV.

docs.title: Restore seems to have lost data
docs.text: Restore replaces all current data with the backup contents. If you had newer data that wasn't in the backup, it's gone. Always export a backup of your current data before restoring from an older one.

docs.title: Touch ID not available
docs.text: Touch ID requires a Mac with a Touch ID sensor (MacBook Pro/Air with Touch Bar or later). On other Macs, use your system password instead.

docs.title: Can't unlock the app
docs.text:
• Try your Mac's system login password — it's always available as a fallback authentication method.
  • If you've changed your system password recently, the new password should work.

docs.title: App keeps locking too quickly
docs.text: Adjust the timeout settings in Settings (Cmd+,) > Security. Set "When Switching Apps" and "When Locked or Sleeping" to longer intervals or "Never".

docs.title: App feels slow with many snapshots
docs.text:
This can happen with very large portfolios (hundreds of assets across many snapshots). Performance depends on your Mac's hardware.

Tip

If you encounter an issue not listed here, try restarting AssetFlow. If the problem persists, you can report it on the project's GitHub page.

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Welcome to AssetFlow! This page walks you through system requirements, installation, and a quick tour of the app so you know where everything lives.

docs.title: System Requirements
docs.text: Requirement Details Operating System macOS 15.0 (Sequoia) or later Architecture Apple silicon or Intel Mac Disk Space Minimal — AssetFlow stores data locally in a lightweight database Network Optional — only used to fetch exchange rates

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
1. Go to the Releases page on GitHub and download the latest AssetFlow-x.y.z.zip.
  2. Unzip the file and move AssetFlow.app into your Applications folder.
  3. Launch AssetFlow from Applications or Spotlight (Cmd+Space and type "AssetFlow").

docs.title: Bypassing the Gatekeeper Warning
docs.text:
Because AssetFlow is not signed with an Apple Developer certificate, macOS will block it on first launch. This is normal — here's how to allow it:

  1. Double-click AssetFlow.app. You'll see a warning that the app cannot be opened.
  2. Open System Settings → Privacy & Security.
  3. Scroll down to the Security section. You'll see a message: "AssetFlow was blocked from use because it is not from an identified developer."
  4. Click Open Anyway, then authenticate with Touch ID or your password.
  5. In the confirmation dialog, click Open.

The app will open normally on all subsequent launches.

Note

This is a one-time step. Once you've allowed AssetFlow through Gatekeeper, macOS will remember your choice and won't ask again.

docs.title: First Launch
docs.text:
When you open AssetFlow for the first time, you'll see an empty dashboard with helpful prompts to get started. There's no account to create and no sign-in required — everything runs locally on your Mac.

From here you can:

  • Create your first snapshot to start tracking your portfolio
  • Import data from a CSV file if you already have your holdings in a spreadsheet

docs.title: App Overview
docs.text: AssetFlow is organized around a sidebar on the left that gives you quick access to every part of the app.

docs.title: Sidebar Navigation
docs.text:
The sidebar is divided into three sections:

Overview Your main dashboard — a summary of your portfolio with charts and performance metrics. Portfolio
  • Snapshots — browse and manage your portfolio snapshots
  • Assets — view and edit all of your tracked assets
  • Categories — organize assets into groups and set target allocations
  • Platforms — manage the platforms or brokerages where your assets are held
Tools
  • Rebalancing — calculate trades needed to reach your target allocation
  • Import CSV — bulk import assets or cash flows from spreadsheet files

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text: Ready to add your first data? Head to the Quick Start guide to create your first snapshot in just a few minutes.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: This guide walks you through two ways to start tracking your portfolio in AssetFlow. Pick the path that fits your situation best.

docs.title: Option A: Import from CSV (Recommended for Existing Portfolios)
docs.text:
If you already have your holdings in a spreadsheet, CSV import is the fastest way to get started.

  1. Open AssetFlow.

  2. Navigate to Tools > Import CSV in the sidebar, or press Cmd+I.

  3. Select Assets as the import type.

  4. Drag and drop your CSV file onto the import area, or click Browse to select it from Finder.

    Expected CSV columns

    Your file should include at least an Asset Name and Market Value column. Platform and Currency columns are optional — if omitted, you can set defaults during import.

  5. Configure the import settings: pick a snapshot date, and optionally assign a default platform and category for the imported assets.

  6. Review the preview table. AssetFlow will highlight any validation errors so you can fix them before importing.

  7. Click Import to bring everything in.

Tip

You can always edit imported assets after the fact. Don't worry about getting everything perfect on the first try.

docs.title: Option B: Create a Snapshot Manually
docs.text:
Prefer to enter things by hand? You can build your portfolio directly in the app.

  1. Navigate to Portfolio > Snapshots in the sidebar.
  2. Click the + button in the toolbar, or press Cmd+N.
  3. Choose a date for the snapshot.
  4. Choose a creation mode:
    • Bulk Entry (default) — a full-screen view where you enter values for all assets at once, grouped by platform. You can create new assets and platforms inline, and import CSV files per platform.
    • Empty Snapshot — a blank snapshot where you add assets one by one in the detail view.
  5. Click Create.

Tip

Bulk Entry is the fastest way to build a snapshot from scratch — you can add platforms and assets inline without switching between screens. See Bulk Entry for a full walkthrough.

Note

Each snapshot captures your portfolio at a single point in time. You'll create new snapshots whenever you want to record an update — for example, at the end of each month.

docs.title: Set Up Categories
docs.text:
Categories help you organize your assets and define your target allocation. Setting them up early makes the rebalancing tool much more useful.

  1. Navigate to Portfolio > Categories in the sidebar.
  2. Click + to create a new category (for example: Equities, Bonds, Cash, Real Estate).
  3. For each category, set a target allocation percentage — these should add up to 100%.
  4. Navigate to Portfolio > Assets and assign each asset to the appropriate category.

Sample categories

Category Target Allocation Equities 60% Bonds 30% Cash 10%

docs.title: Explore the Dashboard
docs.text:
Once you have at least one snapshot with assets, the dashboard comes to life.

  1. Navigate to Overview in the sidebar to see your portfolio summary.
  2. Browse the allocation charts to see how your current holdings compare to your targets.
  3. Check the performance metrics to track growth over time.

Tip

The more snapshots you create over time, the richer your performance charts and metrics become. Try to record a snapshot regularly — monthly is a good cadence for most people.

docs.title: Assets
docs.text: Assets represent individual investments in your portfolio — stocks, bonds, ETFs, crypto, cash accounts, or anything else you want to track. Each asset belongs to a category, is associated with a platform, and has a currency.

docs.title: Asset List
docs.text:
The left side of the Assets view shows all your assets. You can toggle between two grouping modes using the button in the toolbar:

  • By Platform — groups assets under the broker or service where they're held.
  • By Category — groups assets by their investment category.

Each row shows:

  • Asset name
  • Platform
  • Category
  • Latest value (with a currency badge if the asset isn't in your main currency)

Right-click an asset for a context menu. The Delete option is only available if the asset has no values recorded in any snapshot.

docs.title: Asset Detail
docs.text: Select an asset to see its full detail on the right side.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
All properties are editable directly in the detail view:

  • Name — the display name for the asset.
  • Platform — choose from existing platforms or create a new one inline.
  • Category — choose from existing categories or create a new one inline.
  • Currency — select from the list of supported currencies.

docs.title: Value History
docs.text:
The value history section shows how the asset's value has changed over time.

  • Time range selector — choose from All, 1Y, 3M, or 1M to zoom in on a specific period.
  • Line chart — a visual plot of the asset's value over time. Hover over any point to see a tooltip with the exact date and value.
  • Show Converted Value toggle — this only appears for assets in a currency different from your main currency. When enabled, the chart switches to show values converted to your main currency, so you can see how exchange rate changes affected the value.
  • Value history table — below the chart, a table lists every recorded value with columns for Date, Market Value, and Converted Value. Double-click or right-click any row to edit the value for that snapshot.

docs.title: Deleting Assets
docs.text:
Assets can only be deleted when they have no values in any snapshot. If the asset still has recorded values, the delete button will be disabled with an explanation.

To fully remove an asset:

  1. Remove it from every snapshot where it appears (right-click the asset in each snapshot's breakdown and select Remove).
  2. Once the asset has no snapshot values, you can delete it from the asset list.

Note

Assets are typically created through CSV import or by adding them to a snapshot. You don't need to create assets separately — they're created on the fly when you first add them to a snapshot.

docs.text:

  • Categories: Organize assets into groups
  • Platforms: Manage the platforms where your assets are held
  • Import CSV: Bulk-import assets and values from a spreadsheet

docs.title: Bulk Entry
docs.text: Bulk Entry is a full-screen workflow for entering asset values across all platforms in a single session. It's the default way to create a new snapshot, and it's designed to make monthly portfolio updates fast and efficient.

docs.title: Opening Bulk Entry
docs.text:
1. Navigate to Portfolio > Snapshots in the sidebar.
  2. Click the + button in the toolbar or press Cmd+N.
  3. Choose a date for the snapshot.
  4. Select Bulk Entry as the creation mode (this is the default).
  5. Click Create.

The Bulk Entry view opens with all assets from your most recent snapshot, grouped by platform — ready for you to update their values.

Tip

If this is your first snapshot, the view starts empty. Click Add Platform to begin adding assets.

docs.title: The Bulk Entry Table
docs.text:
Assets are organized into collapsible platform sections, sorted alphabetically. Each platform header shows:

  • Platform name (or "No Platform" for unassigned assets)
  • Asset count
  • Progress indicator (e.g., "3/5" means 3 out of 5 assets have been updated)
  • Add Asset button for inline asset creation
  • Import CSV button for per-platform CSV import

docs.title: Columns
docs.text: Column Description Include Checkbox to include or exclude the asset from the snapshot. Excluded assets are dimmed and won't be saved. Asset Name Read-only for existing assets. Editable for newly created assets, with a green "NEW" badge. Category Shows the asset's category. For new assets, a dropdown lets you pick an existing category or create a new one. Currency Read-only for existing assets. A dropdown picker for new assets. Previous Value The asset's value from your most recent prior snapshot, for reference. Shows "—" if no prior value exists. New Value Enter the current market value here. Press Enter to move to the next row.

docs.title: Row States
docs.text:
Rows are color-coded to help you track progress:

  • Green background: The value has been entered or imported (updated).
  • No highlight: The asset is included but hasn't been given a value yet (pending). It will be saved with a value of 0.
  • Dimmed (50% opacity): The asset is excluded and won't be saved.

docs.title: Progress Toolbar
docs.text:
The toolbar at the top shows real-time progress with colored indicators:

  • Green: Updated assets (value entered or imported)
  • Orange: Pending assets (included but no value yet)
  • Gray: Excluded assets

docs.title: Adding a New Asset
docs.text:
Click Add Asset in a platform's header to append a new row. Fill in:

  • Asset Name — must be unique within the platform (case-insensitive)
  • Category — pick from existing categories, or select "New Category…" to create one inline
  • Currency — defaults to your main currency

New assets are marked with a green NEW badge. Use the trash icon to remove a new row if you change your mind.

docs.title: Adding a New Platform
docs.text: Click Add Platform in the toolbar to open the new platform popover. Enter a unique platform name and click Add. A new platform section appears with one empty asset row, ready for editing.

docs.title: Per-Platform CSV Import
docs.text:
Each platform section has an Import CSV button that lets you import values from a CSV file directly into that platform group.

When you import:

  • Matched assets (same name, case-insensitive) have their New Value updated with the CSV value and receive a blue CSV badge.
  • Unmatched assets are added as new rows with a blue CSV badge.

After the import, an alert summarizes the results — how many assets were updated, how many were added, and any rows that were skipped due to platform or currency mismatches.

Note

This per-platform CSV import is separate from the full Import CSV tool (Cmd+I). It's a quick way to pull in values for a single platform without leaving the Bulk Entry view.

docs.title: Validation
docs.text:
The toolbar displays validation warnings that must be resolved before saving:

  • Zero-value assets: "N assets have a value of 0. Exclude them or enter a non-zero value." Fields with a value of 0 are highlighted with a red border.
  • Missing names: "Some new assets are missing a name." New asset rows must have a name before saving.
  • Duplicate names: "Duplicate asset names found within a platform." Both duplicate rows are highlighted with a red border.

The Save Snapshot button is disabled until all validation issues are resolved.

docs.title: Saving the Snapshot
docs.text:
When everything looks good, click Save Snapshot. If any included assets still have no value (pending), a confirmation dialog will ask whether you'd like to save them with a value of 0. You can always update these values later in the snapshot detail view.

After saving, you're taken to the snapshot detail view where you can review the result, add cash flows, or make further edits.

docs.title: Zero-Value Warnings
docs.text:
Assets saved with a value of 0 are flagged throughout the app to remind you to update them:

  • Snapshot detail view: A yellow warning icon appears next to each zero-value asset. Hover over it for details.
  • Snapshot list: A yellow warning icon appears on any snapshot that contains zero-value assets.

docs.title: Cash Flows
docs.text: Cash flows record external money movements — deposits into and withdrawals from your portfolio. Recording these accurately is important because they directly affect how AssetFlow calculates your investment returns.

docs.title: What Counts as a Cash Flow?
docs.text:
• Deposit — money added to your portfolio from outside. For example, a monthly savings contribution or a bonus you decide to invest.
  • Withdrawal — money taken out of your portfolio. For example, selling investments to cover an expense.
  • Not a cash flow — moving money between assets within your portfolio (e.g., selling stocks to buy bonds). These internal transfers don't change your portfolio's total external funding.

docs.title: Adding Cash Flows
docs.text:
1. Navigate to a snapshot's detail view.
  2. Scroll down to the Cash Flow Operations section.
  3. Click Add Cash Flow.
  4. Enter a Description and an Amount — use a positive number for deposits and a negative number for withdrawals.

You can also import cash flows in bulk from a CSV file. See Import CSV for details.

docs.title: Editing and Deleting
docs.text:
• Right-click any cash flow entry to see the context menu.
  • Choose Edit to open a popover where you can change the description and amount.
  • Choose Remove to delete the cash flow.

docs.title: How Cash Flows Affect Returns
docs.text:
Without cash flow tracking, a $10,000 deposit would look like your investments grew by $10,000 — even though that money came from your bank account, not from investment gains. AssetFlow uses your cash flow data to separate actual investment performance from external money movements, giving you an accurate picture of how well your investments are really doing.

Head over to Performance Metrics for a detailed explanation of how returns are calculated.

Tip

Record all deposits and withdrawals for each snapshot period. The more accurate your cash flow data, the more meaningful your performance metrics will be.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Performance Metrics: Understand how cash flows factor into return calculations
  • Snapshots: Create and manage portfolio snapshots
  • Import CSV: Bulk-import cash flows from a spreadsheet

docs.title: Categories
docs.text: Categories let you group your assets by type — such as Equities, Bonds, Real Estate, Cash, or any grouping that makes sense for your portfolio. Each category can have a target allocation to help you with rebalancing.

docs.title: Category List
docs.text:
The left side of the Categories view shows all your categories. You can drag to reorder them — the order you set here is used throughout the app.

Each row displays:

  • Category name
  • Target allocation %
  • Current allocation %
  • Current value
  • Asset count
  • A deviation indicator (warning icon) that appears when the current allocation differs from the target by more than 5%

docs.title: Banners
docs.text:
Two informational banners may appear at the top of the list:

  • Target Allocation Sum Warning — shown if your total target allocations exceed 100%, or if some categories are missing targets. This helps you catch configuration issues.
  • Significant Deviation Info — shown when one or more categories have meaningful deviations from their targets, with a link to the Rebalancing tool.

Right-click a category to delete it (only available if no assets are assigned to it).

docs.title: Creating a Category
docs.text:
Click the + button in the toolbar to create a new category. You'll be asked to enter:

  • Category Name — a descriptive name for the group (e.g., "Equities", "Fixed Income", "Alternatives").
  • Target Allocation % — optional, between 0 and 100. This is the percentage of your portfolio you'd like this category to represent.

docs.title: Category Detail
docs.text: Select a category to see its detail view on the right side.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text:
• Name — editable directly in the detail view.
  • Target Allocation % — editable, with the same 0–100 range.

docs.title: Assets in Category
docs.text: A table showing all assets assigned to this category, with each asset's name and platform.

docs.title: Value History
docs.text: A line chart showing the category's total value over time. Use the time range selector to focus on a specific period.

docs.title: Allocation History
docs.text: A line chart showing how the category's allocation percentage has changed over time. A target line is overlaid for easy comparison, so you can see at a glance how closely your actual allocation has tracked your goal.

docs.title: Deleting a Category
docs.text:
Categories can only be deleted when no assets are assigned to them. If a category still has assets, you'll need to reassign those assets to other categories first, then come back and delete the empty category.

Tip

Use the Rebalancing tool to see at a glance which categories are over or under their target allocation. It's the quickest way to decide where to invest next.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Rebalancing: Compare current allocations to targets
  • Assets: Manage individual investments
  • Dashboard: See category allocation in the pie chart

docs.title: Dashboard
docs.text: The Dashboard is the first thing you see when you open AssetFlow — it's your home screen (found under Overview > Dashboard in the sidebar). It gives you a high-level summary of your entire portfolio at a glance.

docs.title: Summary Cards
docs.text:
At the top of the Dashboard, you'll find a set of summary cards:

  • Total Portfolio Value — the hero card front and center, showing your portfolio's current total value.
  • Latest Snapshot Date — the date of your most recent snapshot.
  • Asset Count — how many assets are in your portfolio.
  • Cumulative TWR — your all-time time-weighted return (the industry-standard measure of investment performance).
  • CAGR — your compound annual growth rate, so you can compare your performance year-over-year.

docs.title: Period Performance Cards
docs.text:
Below the summary, you can select a time period — 1M, 3M, or 1Y — to see how your portfolio performed over that window. Each performance card shows:

  • Growth Rate: The simple percentage change in your portfolio's value. This includes the effect of any deposits or withdrawals you made.
  • Return Rate: The cash-flow-adjusted return using the Modified Dietz method. This isolates your actual investment performance by accounting for when money was added or removed.
  • A date range subtitle showing exactly which period is covered.

Tip

Not sure what the difference between Growth Rate and Return Rate is? Head over to the Performance Metrics page for a plain-language explanation.

docs.title: Charts
docs.text:
The Dashboard includes several interactive charts to help you visualize your portfolio:

  • Category Allocation Pie Chart — see how your portfolio is divided across categories. Click a category slice to jump directly to that category's detail page.
  • Portfolio Value Line Chart — tracks your total portfolio value over time. Click any data point to navigate to that snapshot.
  • Cumulative TWR Line Chart — shows your time-weighted returns over time, so you can see how your investment performance has trended.
  • Category Value Line Chart — tracks how each category's value changes over time, making it easy to spot which parts of your portfolio are growing.

All charts support hover tooltips — just move your cursor over any data point to see the exact date and value.

docs.title: Recent Snapshots
docs.text: At the bottom of the Dashboard, you'll find a list of your latest snapshots. Each entry shows the snapshot date, total value, and asset count. Click any row to jump straight to that snapshot's detail view.

docs.title: Empty State
docs.text: If you haven't created any snapshots yet, the Dashboard will show a friendly prompt to help you get started — either by importing a CSV file or by creating your first snapshot manually.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Performance Metrics: Understand what each metric means
  • Snapshots: Learn how to create and manage snapshots
  • Categories: Organize your assets by type

docs.title: Import CSV
docs.text: AssetFlow's CSV import lets you bulk-import assets and cash flows from spreadsheet exports. This is the fastest way to get started with an existing portfolio or update your holdings in bulk.

docs.title: Opening the Import Tool
docs.text:
Navigate to Tools > Import CSV in the sidebar, or press Cmd+I.

At the top of the import view, toggle between Assets and Cash Flows to choose what you'd like to import.

docs.title: Step 1: Select File
docs.text:
Drag and drop a CSV file onto the import area, or click Browse to select one from Finder.

Your file should include at least these columns:

Column Required Description Asset Name Yes The name of the asset Market Value Yes The current market value Platform No Which platform holds this asset Currency No The asset's currency code (e.g., USD, TWD)

docs.title: Step 2: Configure
docs.text:
Before importing, you can fine-tune how the data is brought in:

  • Snapshot Date — choose which date this snapshot represents.
  • Platform — optionally assign a platform. You can create a new platform inline if needed. Check All Rows to apply it to every imported asset, or leave it unchecked to only fill in rows that don't already have a platform from the CSV.
  • Category — optionally assign a category. You can create a new category inline as well. The All Rows checkbox works the same way — check it to override all rows, or leave it unchecked to only fill blanks.
  • Copy Forward — toggle this on to carry forward assets from other platforms that aren't included in this CSV. This is useful when you're importing from one brokerage but want to keep assets from others in the same snapshot.
    • Select which platforms to copy forward from.
    • Each option shows the asset count and source snapshot date.

docs.title: Step 3: Review & Import
docs.text:
The preview table shows all parsed rows along with their validation status:

  • Errors (red) — these block the import and must be fixed. Common causes include missing required columns, duplicate assets, or unparsable values.
  • Warnings (yellow) — these allow the import but flag potential issues, such as zero values or unrecognized currency codes.

You can remove problematic rows directly from the preview before importing.

When everything looks good, click Import.

Warning

If a snapshot already exists for the selected date, the import will update it rather than creating a new one. Assets with a value of 0 in the existing snapshot will be overwritten by the imported values.

docs.title: Importing Cash Flows
docs.text:
The cash flow import follows a similar workflow. Your CSV should include:

Column Required Description Description Yes A label for the cash flow (e.g., "Monthly deposit") Amount Yes Positive for deposits, negative for withdrawals Currency No The currency code

Cash flows are imported into a specific snapshot that you select during configuration.

Tip

You can also import CSV files per platform directly from the Bulk Entry view — handy when you want to update values for a single platform without leaving the snapshot creation workflow. See Bulk Entry for details.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• CSV Format Reference: Detailed column specifications and example files
  • Snapshots: Understand how snapshots work
  • Bulk Entry: Enter values for all assets in a single session, with per-platform CSV import
  • Cash Flows: Learn more about tracking deposits and withdrawals

docs.title: Performance Metrics
docs.text: AssetFlow calculates several performance metrics to help you understand how your investments are doing. If you've ever wondered what all those numbers on the Dashboard mean, this page breaks each one down in plain language.

docs.title: Growth Rate
docs.text:
What it is: The simple percentage change in your portfolio's total value between two dates.

How it works: Take the difference between your ending value and starting value, divide by the starting value, and multiply by 100%. That's your growth rate.

Important: Growth rate includes the effect of deposits and withdrawals. If you deposited $10,000 during the period, your portfolio will show growth from that deposit even if your investments stayed completely flat.

Best for: A quick overview of how your total wealth changed — useful when you want to see the big picture, including the money you added or removed.

docs.title: Return Rate (Modified Dietz)
docs.text:
What it is: A cash-flow-adjusted return that isolates your investment performance from the effect of deposits and withdrawals.

How it works: The Modified Dietz method accounts for when money was added or removed during the period, weighting each cash flow by how long it was actually invested. Money deposited at the start of the month counts more than money deposited at the end.

Why it matters: If you deposit $10,000 mid-month, the Modified Dietz method won't count that deposit as "growth." It only measures how well your existing money (and newly added money, from the time it was added) actually performed.

Best for: Understanding your actual investment performance over a single period. This is the number to look at when you want to know "how did my investments do this month?" without being misled by deposits or withdrawals.

docs.title: Cumulative Time-Weighted Return (TWR)
docs.text:
What it is: Chains together the returns from each snapshot period to show your total investment performance over time, completely eliminating the impact of cash flow timing.

How it works: AssetFlow calculates the return between each consecutive pair of snapshots (using the Modified Dietz method), then compounds them together — like multiplying each period's growth factor. The result shows your overall investment performance as if cash flows never happened.

Why it matters: Even if you made a large deposit right before a market dip, the TWR measures how your investment choices performed — not your deposit timing. Two people with identical investments but different deposit schedules will see the same TWR.

Best for: Long-term performance tracking. This is the industry-standard way to evaluate investment performance, and it's the number fund managers and financial advisors use.

docs.title: CAGR (Compound Annual Growth Rate)
docs.text:
What it is: The annualized version of your cumulative TWR — it tells you what your portfolio would have grown at per year if growth were perfectly smooth and steady.

Best for: Comparing your portfolio's performance to benchmarks. When someone says "the S&P 500 returned 10% per year," that's a CAGR — and now you can compare your own portfolio on the same terms.

Tip

If you only look at one number, make it the Cumulative TWR — it's the fairest measure of how well your investments are actually performing.

Note

For all these metrics to be accurate, make sure to record your cash flows (deposits and withdrawals) in each snapshot. Without cash flow data, AssetFlow can't distinguish between investment gains and money you added.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Dashboard: See these metrics in action
  • Cash Flows: Learn how to record deposits and withdrawals

docs.title: Platforms
docs.text:
Platforms represent where your assets are held — brokerage accounts, exchanges, banks, or any other custodian. They help you track which assets are on which platform, so you always know where your money lives.

Most of the time, you won't need to create platforms by hand. They're typically auto-created when you import a CSV file or assign a platform while adding an asset.

docs.title: Platform List
docs.text:
The left side of the Platforms view shows all your platforms in a sortable list.

  • Drag to reorder — arrange platforms in whatever order makes sense to you.
  • Each row shows the platform name, total value, and asset count.
  • Right-click any platform to rename it.

docs.title: Platform Detail
docs.text:
Select a platform from the list to see its detail view on the right side.

  • Name — displayed at the top. Right-click the platform in the list to rename it.
  • Assets on Platform — a table listing every asset held on this platform, along with each asset's name and category.
  • Value History — a line chart showing the platform's total value over time. Use the time range selector to zoom in on a specific period, and hover over any data point to see the exact date and value.

Note

You don't need to create platforms manually. They are automatically created when you import CSV data or assign a platform to an asset.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Assets: Manage individual assets and assign them to platforms
  • Import CSV: Bulk-import assets with platform information from a spreadsheet

docs.title: Rebalancing
docs.text:
The Rebalancing tool shows you exactly how your current portfolio allocation compares to your targets and suggests what to buy or sell to get back on track.

Note

AssetFlow's rebalancing tool is a read-only calculator. It shows suggestions — you'll need to execute any trades yourself through your brokerage.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text: Rebalancing compares your current allocation (based on the latest snapshot) against the target allocations you've set on your categories. It calculates the difference and tells you how much to buy or sell in each category to reach your targets.

docs.title: Reading the Rebalancing Table
docs.text:
At the top, you'll see your Total Portfolio Value.

Below that, the table is divided into three sections:

  1. Categories with Targets — the main rebalancing view, showing categories where you've defined a target allocation.
  2. No Target Set — categories that exist but don't have a target allocation assigned.
  3. Uncategorized — assets that haven't been assigned to any category.

Each row in the table includes:

Column Description Category The category name Current Value The total value of assets in this category Current % This category's share of your portfolio Target % Your desired allocation for this category Difference The gap between current and target allocation Action What you need to do to reach your target

Actions are color-coded for quick scanning:

  • Buy (green) — the amount you need to purchase to reach your target.
  • Sell (red) — the amount you need to sell to reduce to your target.
  • No Action (gray) — you're already at your target. Nice work!

docs.title: Suggested Moves
docs.text: At the bottom of the view, you'll find a Suggested Moves summary with natural-language descriptions of the key actions needed to bring your portfolio back into balance.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text:
To use the rebalancing tool, you'll need:

  • At least one snapshot with assets.
  • Categories with target allocations set.
  • Assets assigned to categories.

Tip

Start by setting target allocations on your categories (e.g., Equities 60%, Bonds 30%, Cash 10%). Then the rebalancing tool will automatically calculate what you need to do.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Categories: Create categories and set target allocations
  • Assets: Assign assets to categories
  • Dashboard: See your allocation breakdown at a glance

docs.title: Snapshots
docs.text: Snapshots are the core concept in AssetFlow. Each snapshot captures your entire portfolio's state at a specific point in time — like a photograph of your finances. By comparing snapshots over time, AssetFlow can calculate your performance, track allocation changes, and show you how your portfolio evolves.

docs.title: Snapshot List
docs.text:
The left side of the Snapshots view shows all your snapshots, grouped by time period: This Month, Previous 3 Months, Previous 6 Months, Previous Year, and Older.

Each row displays:

  • Snapshot date
  • Platform badges (up to 3 visible, with a +N overflow indicator if you have more)
  • Total portfolio value
  • Asset count

Right-click any snapshot for a context menu with the option to Delete it.

docs.title: Creating a New Snapshot
docs.text:
Click the + button in the toolbar or press Cmd+N to create a new snapshot.

You'll be asked to:

  1. Choose a date — the snapshot date cannot be in the future, and you can't have two snapshots on the same date. If the selected date already has a snapshot, a warning appears and the Create button is disabled.
  2. Choose a creation mode:
    • Bulk Entry (default) — opens a full-screen view where you can enter values for all your assets at once, grouped by platform. Previous values are carried forward for reference. This is the recommended workflow for monthly updates.
    • Empty Snapshot — creates a blank snapshot and takes you to the snapshot detail view, where you can add assets one by one.

Tip

Use Bulk Entry for monthly updates — all your assets are pre-loaded from the latest snapshot, so you just update the values that changed. See Bulk Entry for a full walkthrough.

docs.title: Snapshot Detail
docs.text: The right side shows the detail view for the selected snapshot.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
At the top, you'll see:

  • Total Value — the sum of all assets in this snapshot. A yellow warning icon appears if the snapshot contains assets with a value of 0
  • Net Cash Flow — total deposits minus withdrawals, with the number of operations shown
  • Exchange Rate Status — whether rates have been fetched for this snapshot's date

docs.title: Asset Breakdown
docs.text:
A table listing every asset in the snapshot with its market value. For multi-currency assets, both the original currency value and the converted value are shown.

  • Right-click an asset to edit its value or remove it from the snapshot.
  • Add Asset — click to add an existing asset to this snapshot, or create a new asset on the spot.

docs.title: Category Allocation
docs.text: Shows all categories represented in this snapshot, with their values and percentage of the total portfolio.

docs.title: Exchange Rates
docs.text: Displays the exchange rates used for currency conversions. Rates are auto-fetched when you create a snapshot. If fetching failed, you'll see a Retry button to try again.

docs.title: Cash Flow Operations
docs.text: Lists all deposits and withdrawals recorded for this snapshot. Right-click any entry to edit or remove it. Use the Add Cash Flow button to record a new deposit or withdrawal.

docs.title: Editing Asset Values
docs.text:
There are two ways to edit an asset's value within a snapshot:

  1. Right-click the asset in the breakdown and select Edit Value.
  2. Double-click the value directly.

A popover will appear where you can enter the new market value.

docs.title: Deleting a Snapshot
docs.text:
To delete a snapshot, scroll to the Danger Zone at the bottom of the snapshot detail view and click Delete Snapshot. A confirmation dialog will appear showing the snapshot date, asset count, and cash flow count so you know exactly what will be removed.

Warning

Deleting a snapshot permanently removes all recorded values and cash flows for that date. The assets themselves are not deleted — they will still exist and appear in other snapshots.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Bulk Entry: Enter values for all assets in a single session
  • Cash Flows: Record deposits and withdrawals for each snapshot
  • Assets: Manage your individual investments
  • Import CSV: Bulk-import snapshot data from a spreadsheet

docs.title: CSV Format
docs.text: Reference for the CSV file formats accepted by AssetFlow's import tool.

docs.title: Required Columns
docs.text: Column Description Asset Name The name of the asset (e.g., "AAPL", "Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF") Market Value The current market value as a number (e.g., 15000, 1234.56)

docs.title: Optional Columns
docs.text: Column Description Platform The platform/brokerage where the asset is held Currency Three-letter currency code (e.g., USD, EUR, TWD). Defaults to your main currency if omitted.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Asset Name,Market Value,Platform,Currency
AAPL,15000,Interactive Brokers,USD
VTI,28000,Interactive Brokers,USD
Bitcoin,5000,Coinbase,USD
台積電,120000,永豐金證券,TWD

docs.title: Required Columns
docs.text: Column Description Description A label for the cash flow (e.g., "Monthly savings", "Dividend reinvestment") Amount The amount as a number. Positive = deposit, negative = withdrawal.

docs.title: Optional Columns
docs.text: Column Description Currency Three-letter currency code. Defaults to your main currency if omitted.

docs.title: Example
docs.text:
Description,Amount,Currency
Monthly savings,5000,USD
Emergency withdrawal,-2000,USD
年終獎金投入,100000,TWD

docs.title: Common Issues
docs.text:
Issue Cause Solution "Missing required column" CSV doesn't have Asset Name or Market Value header Check column headers match exactly "Duplicate asset" Same Asset Name + Platform combination appears twice Remove duplicate rows or change the platform "Unparsable value" Market Value contains non-numeric characters Remove currency symbols, commas in numbers "Unsupported currency" Currency code not recognized Use standard ISO 4217 codes (e.g., USD, EUR, TWD)

Tip

Export your spreadsheet as UTF-8 CSV to ensure special characters (like Chinese) are handled correctly.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Import CSV: Step-by-step guide for importing data
  • Currencies & Exchange Rates: Supported currencies and how exchange rates work

docs.title: Currencies & Exchange Rates
docs.text: AssetFlow supports multiple currencies and automatically fetches exchange rates to convert all values to your main display currency.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
• Each asset can be tracked in its own currency (e.g., US stocks in USD, Taiwanese stocks in TWD).
  • Exchange rates are fetched automatically for each snapshot date.
  • All values are converted to your main currency for display. You can change your main currency in Settings (Cmd+,) > Preferences.
  • Source: Exchange rates are fetched from cdn.jsdelivr.net, which provides open-source rate data.

docs.title: Supported Currencies
docs.text: AssetFlow ships with a built-in list of common currencies. On first launch (and periodically thereafter), the app fetches an expanded currency list from the exchange rate API and caches it locally for offline use.

docs.title: Built-in Currencies
docs.text:
These currencies are always available, even without an internet connection:

Code Currency Code Currency AED UAE Dirham KRW South Korean Won AUD Australian Dollar MXN Mexican Peso BRL Brazilian Real MYR Malaysian Ringgit BTC Bitcoin NOK Norwegian Krone CAD Canadian Dollar NZD New Zealand Dollar CHF Swiss Franc PHP Philippine Peso CNY Chinese Yuan PLN Polish Zloty DKK Danish Krone SAR Saudi Riyal ETH Ethereum SEK Swedish Krona EUR Euro SGD Singapore Dollar GBP British Pound THB Thai Baht HKD Hong Kong Dollar TRY Turkish Lira IDR Indonesian Rupiah TWD New Taiwan Dollar ILS Israeli Shekel USD US Dollar INR Indian Rupee VND Vietnamese Dong JPY Japanese Yen ZAR South African Rand

docs.title: Full Currency List
docs.text:
After the first successful network connection, AssetFlow fetches the complete list of available currencies from the exchange rate API and caches it locally. This includes hundreds of fiat and crypto currencies beyond the built-in list above.

You can view the full list of supported currency codes by opening the API endpoint in your browser.

docs.title: Auto-Fetch Behavior
docs.text:
Exchange rates are fetched automatically in several situations:

  • New snapshot — when you create a snapshot that includes assets in multiple currencies, rates for that date are fetched immediately.
  • App launch — any missing rates for existing snapshots are fetched in the background.
  • After restore — when you restore from a backup, rates are re-fetched for all snapshots.

docs.title: When Rates Can't Be Fetched
docs.text:
If you're offline or experiencing network issues:

  • The app shows a Retry button in the snapshot detail view so you can try again later.
  • Values are shown unconverted, in their original currencies.
  • Everything else in the app works fully offline — exchange rates are the only network feature.

Note

AssetFlow stores fetched exchange rates locally. Once rates are fetched for a snapshot, they don't need to be re-fetched even when offline.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Preferences: Set your main display currency
  • Assets: Manage assets and their currencies
  • CSV Format: Specify currencies in CSV imports

docs.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
docs.text:
Quick reference for keyboard shortcuts in AssetFlow.

Shortcut Action Cmd+N Create new snapshot Cmd+I Open CSV import Cmd+, Open Settings Enter Move to next row's New Value (in Bulk Entry)

Note

More keyboard shortcuts may be added in future updates.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Snapshots: Learn about creating and managing snapshots
  • Bulk Entry: Enter values for all assets in a single session
  • Import CSV: Bulk-import data from spreadsheets

docs.title: Backup & Restore
docs.text: Protect your portfolio data by regularly creating backups. AssetFlow exports all your data to a single ZIP file that can be restored at any time.

docs.title: Creating a Backup
docs.text:
1. Open Settings (Cmd+,) and go to the Data Management section.
  2. Click Export Backup.
  3. Choose a location and filename in the save dialog.
  4. A ZIP file is created containing all your portfolio data.

docs.title: What's Included
docs.text:
A backup file contains everything in your portfolio:

  • All snapshots and their asset values
  • All assets, categories, and platforms
  • All cash flow operations
  • Exchange rate data
  • App settings

docs.title: Restoring from Backup
docs.text:
1. Open Settings (Cmd+,) and go to the Data Management section.
  2. Click Restore from Backup.
  3. Select a backup ZIP file.
  4. Confirm the restore — this replaces all current data.
  5. After restore, exchange rates are automatically re-fetched for all snapshots.

Warning

Restoring from a backup replaces ALL current data. This action cannot be undone. Make sure to export a backup of your current data first if you want to keep it.

Tip

Create regular backups, especially before major changes to your portfolio data. Store backups in a safe location like an external drive or cloud storage.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Preferences: Customize display and import settings
  • Security: Protect your data with app lock and authentication

docs.title: Preferences
docs.text: Customize how AssetFlow displays and handles your data. Open Settings from the menu bar (AssetFlow > Settings) or press Cmd+,.

docs.title: Main Currency
docs.text:
Choose the currency used to display all portfolio values across the app. When your portfolio includes assets in different currencies, AssetFlow converts everything to this main currency using fetched exchange rates.

Select your preferred currency from the dropdown. See Currencies & Exchange Rates for the full list of supported currencies.

docs.title: Date Format
docs.text: Choose how dates are displayed throughout the app. Several format options are available, and a live preview shows you exactly how dates will appear so you can pick the style that feels most natural.

docs.title: Import Defaults
docs.text: Set a default platform name that pre-fills during CSV import. If you frequently import from the same brokerage or platform, this saves you from typing the same name every time.

docs.title: About
docs.text:
The About section shows:

  • App version and build number
  • Developer info and license
  • Privacy information:
    • All data is stored locally on your Mac
    • Exchange rates are fetched from cdn.jsdelivr.net
    • No personal data is collected or transmitted
  • Source code — a link to the project's GitHub repository

Tip

If you switch your main currency, all portfolio values and charts update immediately to reflect the new currency. Exchange rates are used for the conversion, so make sure rates have been fetched for your snapshots.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Security: Protect your data with app lock and authentication
  • Backup & Restore: Export and import your portfolio data
  • Currencies & Exchange Rates: How multi-currency support works

docs.title: Security
docs.text: AssetFlow offers an optional app lock to protect your financial data from unauthorized access. When enabled, a full-screen overlay hides all portfolio data until you authenticate.

docs.title: Enabling App Lock
docs.text:
1. Open Settings (Cmd+,) and go to the Security section.
  2. Toggle Require Authentication on.

docs.title: Authentication Methods
docs.text:
The available authentication methods depend on your Mac's capabilities:

  • Touch ID — available on MacBooks with a Touch ID sensor (MacBook Pro/Air with Touch Bar or later)
  • Apple Watch — available if you have a paired Apple Watch
  • System password — always available as a fallback on any Mac

docs.title: Timeout Settings
docs.text: Control when AssetFlow locks itself with two independent timeout settings:

docs.title: When Switching Apps
docs.text:
How quickly the app locks after you switch away (e.g., Cmd+Tab to another app).

Option Behavior Immediately Locks the moment you leave AssetFlow 1 Min Locks after 1 minute away 5 Min Locks after 5 minutes away Never Stays unlocked until your Mac sleeps or locks

docs.title: When Locked or Sleeping
docs.text:
How quickly the app locks when your Mac's screen locks or enters sleep mode.

Option Behavior Immediately Locks as soon as the screen locks or sleeps 1 Min Locks after 1 minute of screen lock/sleep 5 Min Locks after 5 minutes of screen lock/sleep Never Stays unlocked even through sleep cycles

Tip

Set "When Switching Apps" to "Never" if you frequently switch between AssetFlow and a spreadsheet while updating values.

docs.title: Lock Screen
docs.text:
When locked, a full-screen overlay completely hides all portfolio data. To unlock:

  1. Click Unlock on the lock screen.
  2. Authenticate using Touch ID, Apple Watch, or your system password.

The app automatically attempts to unlock when it becomes active, so in many cases you just need to look at your Touch ID sensor or tap your Apple Watch.

Note

When the app is locked, tooltips and hover effects are also hidden to prevent data leakage through the lock overlay.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Preferences: Customize display and import settings
  • Backup & Restore: Protect your data with regular backups

docs.title: AssetFlow
docs.text:
一款用於快照式投資組合管理與資產配置追蹤的 macOS 應用程式。

AssetFlow 讓你透過定期記錄投資組合快照，掌握自己的投資狀況。不需要串接券商帳戶，只要在你方便的時候擷取投資組合的當下狀態，就能清楚了解資產配置與成長趨勢。所有資料都存放在你的 Mac 上，不需要任何雲端帳號。

docs.title: 功能特色
docs.text:
• 快照式追蹤

    隨時記錄你的投資組合狀態。比較不同時間的快照，觀察持倉在數週、數月或數年間的變化。

  • 多幣別支援

    支援以多種貨幣追蹤資產，並自動取得匯率。所有數值統一換算為你的主要幣別，一目了然。

  • 類別與目標配置

    將資產歸類為股票、債券、現金等類別，並設定目標配置比例，打造理想的投資組合。

  • 再平衡計算機

    精確告訴你該買進或賣出多少，才能達到目標配置。讓投資組合再平衡不再靠猜測。

  • CSV 匯入

    從試算表批次匯入資產與現金流。利用你現有的資料，快速開始使用。

  • 績效指標

    追蹤成長率、修正 Dietz 報酬率、時間加權報酬率（TWR）以及年化複合成長率（CAGR）。

  • 圖表與視覺化

    互動式圓餅圖呈現配置比例，折線圖呈現投資組合價值走勢。

  • 安全防護

    可選的應用程式鎖定功能，支援 Touch ID、Apple Watch 或密碼驗證，守護你的財務隱私。

docs.title: 說明
docs.text:
• 常見問題：常見問題解答
  • 疑難排解：常見問題的解決方法

docs.title: 什麼是「快照」？
docs.text: 快照是你整個投資組合在某個特定時間點的記錄。就像是你的財務在某個日期的照片。每筆快照儲存你在那個時刻擁有的每項資產的市場價值。

docs.title: 我應該多久建立一次快照？
docs.text: 大多數使用者以每月一次為起點。有些人偏好每週甚至每天記錄。快照建立得越頻繁，你的歷史資料和績效圖表就會越詳細。

docs.title: 成長率和報酬率有什麼差別？
docs.text: 成長率是投資組合總值的簡單百分比變化，包含存入與提領的影響。報酬率使用修正 Dietz 方法將實際投資表現與現金流分開，讓你更準確地了解投資的真正表現。詳細說明請參閱績效指標。

docs.title: 我的資料存放在哪裡？
docs.text: 所有資料都儲存在你的 Mac 本機，使用 Apple 的 SwiftData 框架。沒有任何資料會傳送到外部伺服器。唯一的網路活動是從 cdn.jsdelivr.net 取得匯率。

docs.title: AssetFlow 可以離線使用嗎？
docs.text: 可以。唯一需要網路的功能是匯率取得。其他所有功能都可以完全離線使用。

docs.title: 如何追蹤不同幣別的資產？
docs.text: 在資產的詳細檢視中設定該資產的幣別。AssetFlow 會自動取得匯率並將數值換算為你的主要幣別顯示。詳情請參閱幣別與匯率。

docs.title: 可以從券商匯入資料嗎？
docs.text: 如果你的券商可以匯出 CSV 檔案，就可以。將你的持倉匯出為 CSV，確認檔案有「Asset Name」和「Market Value」欄位，然後使用匯入 CSV 工具即可。預期的格式請參閱 CSV 格式。

docs.title: 刪除快照會怎樣？
docs.text: 該快照及其所有記錄值與現金流會被永久刪除。資產本身會保留，只是不再有該日期的數值。

docs.title: 為什麼我無法刪除某項資產？
docs.text: 資產只有在所有快照中都沒有記錄值時才能刪除。請先從所有快照中移除該資產，然後才能刪除。

docs.title: 我的資料有備份嗎？
docs.text: 不會自動備份。開啟設定（Cmd+,），使用資料管理 > 匯出備份來建立備份 ZIP 檔案。詳情請參閱備份與還原。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 疑難排解：常見問題的解決方法
  • 開始使用：首次設定 AssetFlow

docs.title: 疑難排解
docs.text: 使用 AssetFlow 時可能遇到的常見問題及其解決方法。

docs.title: 匯率無法載入
docs.text:
• 檢查你的網路連線。
  • 在快照詳細檢視中點選重試按鈕。
  • 匯率從 cdn.jsdelivr.net 取得，請確認你的網路可以連線到此網域。
  • 如果你使用防火牆或 VPN，可能會阻擋連線。

docs.title: 匯率顯示過時或不正確
docs.text:
• 匯率是針對特定的快照日期取得的。歷史匯率反映的是該日期的實際匯率，而非今天的匯率。
  • 如果匯率看起來有誤，可能是因為資料來源使用的是收盤匯率，與你在其他地方看到的盤中匯率可能不同。

docs.title: 匯入按鈕無法點選
docs.text: 預覽中有驗證錯誤。查找紅色的錯誤標記並修正問題。常見原因包括缺少欄位、重複的資產，或無法解析的數值。

docs.title: 匯入後文字出現亂碼
docs.text: 你的 CSV 檔案可能不是以 UTF-8 編碼儲存。請從試算表應用程式重新匯出，選擇 UTF-8 編碼。

docs.title: 「重複的資產」錯誤
docs.text: 目標快照中已存在相同名稱和平台的資產。你可以重新命名資產、變更平台，或從 CSV 中移除重複的列。

docs.title: 還原後似乎遺失了資料
docs.text: 還原會以備份內容取代所有目前的資料。如果你有比備份更新的資料但不在備份中，那些資料就會消失。請務必在從舊備份還原之前，先匯出目前資料的備份。

docs.title: Touch ID 無法使用
docs.text: Touch ID 需要配備 Touch ID 感應器的 Mac（MacBook Pro/Air 含 Touch Bar 或更新款式）。在其他 Mac 上，請改用系統密碼。

docs.title: 無法解鎖應用程式
docs.text:
• 試試你的 Mac 系統登入密碼，它永遠可以作為備用驗證方式。
  • 如果你最近變更了系統密碼，新密碼應該可以使用。

docs.title: 應用程式鎖定得太快
docs.text: 在設定（Cmd+,）> 安全性中調整逾時設定。將「切換應用程式時」和「螢幕鎖定或睡眠時」設為較長的間隔或「永不」。

docs.title: 快照很多時應用程式變慢
docs.text:
大型投資組合（有數百項資產橫跨多筆快照）可能會出現這種情況。效能取決於你的 Mac 硬體。

Tip

如果你遇到這裡沒有列出的問題，請嘗試重新啟動 AssetFlow。如果問題持續存在，你可以在專案的 GitHub 頁面上回報。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 常見問題：常見問題解答
  • 偏好設定：自訂應用程式設定

docs.title: 開始使用
docs.text: 歡迎使用 AssetFlow！這個頁面會帶你了解系統需求、安裝方式，以及應用程式的快速導覽，讓你知道每個功能在哪裡。

docs.title: 系統需求
docs.text: 需求 說明 作業系統 macOS 15.0（Sequoia）或更新版本 架構 Apple Silicon 或 Intel Mac 磁碟空間 極小，AssetFlow 使用輕量級資料庫在本機儲存資料 網路 選用，僅用於取得匯率

docs.title: 安裝
docs.text:
1. 前往 GitHub 上的 Releases 頁面，下載最新的 AssetFlow-x.y.z.zip。
  2. 解壓縮檔案，將 AssetFlow.app 移至應用程式資料夾。
  3. 從應用程式或 Spotlight（Cmd+Space 輸入「AssetFlow」）啟動 AssetFlow。

docs.title: 略過 Gatekeeper 警告
docs.text:
由於 AssetFlow 未使用 Apple 開發者憑證簽署，macOS 會在首次啟動時阻擋它。這是正常現象，以下是允許開啟的步驟：

  1. 雙擊 AssetFlow.app，你會看到一個無法開啟應用程式的警告。
  2. 打開系統設定 → 隱私權與安全性。
  3. 向下捲動到安全性區段，你會看到一段訊息：「AssetFlow 因為不是來自已識別的開發者而遭到阻擋。」
  4. 點選仍要打開，然後使用 Touch ID 或密碼進行驗證。
  5. 在確認對話框中，點選打開。

之後每次開啟都不會再出現警告。

Note

這是一次性的步驟。一旦你透過 Gatekeeper 允許了 AssetFlow，macOS 會記住你的選擇，不會再次詢問。

docs.title: 首次啟動
docs.text:
第一次開啟 AssetFlow 時，你會看到一個空白的儀表板，以及引導你開始使用的提示。不需要建立帳號，也不需要登入，所有功能都在你的 Mac 上本機運作。

從這裡你可以：

  • 建立你的第一筆快照，開始追蹤投資組合
  • 從 CSV 檔案匯入資料，如果你已經有試算表中的持倉資料

docs.title: 應用程式總覽
docs.text: AssetFlow 以左側的側邊欄為核心，讓你快速存取應用程式的每個部分。

docs.title: 側邊欄導覽
docs.text:
側邊欄分為三個區段：

總覽 你的主要儀表板：包含圖表與績效指標的投資組合摘要。 投資組合
  • 快照：瀏覽與管理投資組合快照
  • 資產：檢視與編輯所有追蹤的資產
  • 類別：將資產分組並設定目標配置
  • 平台：管理持有資產的平台或券商
工具
  • 再平衡：計算達到目標配置所需的交易
  • 匯入 CSV：從試算表檔案批次匯入資產或現金流

docs.title: 下一步
docs.text: 準備好新增你的第一筆資料了嗎？前往 快速入門 指南，幾分鐘內就能建立你的第一筆快照。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 快速入門：建立第一筆快照的逐步指南
  • 儀表板：認識總覽畫面
  • 匯入 CSV：從試算表批次匯入資料

docs.title: 快速入門
docs.text: 本指南將帶你了解在 AssetFlow 中開始追蹤投資組合的兩種方式，選擇最適合你的方法。

docs.title: 方式 A：從 CSV 匯入（推薦給已有投資組合的使用者）
docs.text:
如果你已經有試算表中的持倉資料，CSV 匯入是最快的上手方式。

  1. 開啟 AssetFlow。

  2. 在側邊欄中前往工具 > 匯入 CSV，或按 Cmd+I。

  3. 選擇資產作為匯入類型。

  4. 將 CSV 檔案拖放到匯入區域，或點選瀏覽從 Finder 中選取。

    預期的 CSV 欄位

    你的檔案至少應包含資產名稱與市場價值欄位。平台與幣別欄位為選填，如果省略，可以在匯入時設定預設值。

  5. 設定匯入選項：選擇快照日期，並可選擇為匯入的資產指定預設的平台與類別。

  6. 檢視預覽表格。AssetFlow 會標示所有驗證錯誤，讓你在匯入前修正。

  7. 點選匯入，將所有資料帶入。

Tip

匯入後隨時都可以編輯資產。第一次不用太追求完美。

docs.title: 方式 B：手動建立快照
docs.text:
偏好手動輸入？你可以直接在應用程式中建立投資組合。

  1. 在側邊欄中前往投資組合 > 快照。
  2. 點選工具列的 + 按鈕，或按 Cmd+N。
  3. 選擇快照的日期。
  4. 選擇建立模式：
    • 批次輸入（預設）：全螢幕檢視，讓你一次輸入所有資產的值，依平台分組。你可以直接建立新資產和平台，也可以針對個別平台匯入 CSV 檔案。
    • 空白快照：建立空白快照，在詳細檢視中逐一新增資產。
  5. 點選建立。

Tip

批次輸入是從頭建立快照最快的方式，你可以直接在畫面中新增平台與資產，無需切換不同畫面。詳細操作請參閱批次輸入。

Note

每筆快照記錄的是你的投資組合在某個時間點的狀態。當你想要記錄更新時就建立新的快照，例如每個月底記錄一次。

docs.title: 設定類別
docs.text:
類別幫助你組織資產並定義目標配置。提早設定好類別，能讓再平衡工具發揮更大的效用。

  1. 在側邊欄中前往投資組合 > 類別。
  2. 點選 + 建立新類別（例如：股票、債券、現金、不動產）。
  3. 為每個類別設定目標配置百分比，所有類別的目標加總應等於 100%。
  4. 前往投資組合 > 資產，將每個資產指定到相應的類別。

範例類別

類別 目標配置 股票 60% 債券 30% 現金 10%

docs.title: 探索儀表板
docs.text:
一旦你有了至少一筆包含資產的快照，儀表板就會開始運作。

  1. 在側邊欄中前往總覽，查看投資組合摘要。
  2. 瀏覽配置圖表，了解目前的持倉與目標的對比。
  3. 查看績效指標，追蹤長期的成長趨勢。

Tip

隨著時間累積越多快照，你的績效圖表與指標就會越豐富。建議定期記錄快照，大多數人每月記錄一次是不錯的頻率。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 儀表板：深入了解總覽畫面
  • 匯入 CSV：詳細的 CSV 匯入指南與格式說明
  • 類別：管理類別與目標配置
  • 快照：管理投資組合快照

docs.title: 資產
docs.text: 資產代表你投資組合中的個別投資標的，例如股票、債券、ETF、加密貨幣、現金帳戶，或是任何你想追蹤的項目。每項資產屬於一個類別、關聯一個平台，並有對應的幣別。

docs.title: 資產列表
docs.text:
資產檢視的左側列出所有資產。你可以使用工具列上的按鈕切換兩種分組模式：

  • 依平台：將資產依持有的券商或服務分組。
  • 依類別：將資產依投資類別分組。

每一列顯示：

  • 資產名稱
  • 平台
  • 類別
  • 最新價值（如果資產的幣別與你的主要幣別不同，會顯示幣別標籤）

在資產上按右鍵可開啟快捷選單。只有當資產在所有快照中都沒有記錄值時，才能選擇刪除。

docs.title: 資產詳細檢視
docs.text: 選取一項資產，即可在右側查看完整的詳細資訊。

docs.title: 屬性
docs.text:
所有屬性都可以直接在詳細檢視中編輯：

  • 名稱：資產的顯示名稱。
  • 平台：從現有平台中選擇，或直接建立新平台。
  • 類別：從現有類別中選擇，或直接建立新類別。
  • 幣別：從支援的幣別清單中選擇。

docs.title: 價值歷史
docs.text:
價值歷史區段顯示資產的價值隨時間的變化。

  • 時間範圍選擇器：從全部、1Y、3M 或 1M 中選擇，聚焦特定期間。
  • 折線圖：資產價值隨時間變化的視覺化圖表。將游標懸停在任一資料點上，可查看確切的日期與數值。
  • 顯示換算值切換：僅在資產的幣別與主要幣別不同時出現。啟用後，圖表會切換顯示換算為主要幣別的值，讓你了解匯率變動對價值的影響。
  • 價值歷史表格：圖表下方有一個表格，列出每筆記錄值，包含日期、市場價值與換算值欄位。雙擊或按右鍵任一列，可編輯該快照的值。

docs.title: 刪除資產
docs.text:
資產只有在所有快照中都沒有記錄值時才能刪除。如果資產仍有記錄值，刪除按鈕會被停用並顯示說明。

若要完全移除一項資產：

  1. 從每一筆包含該資產的快照中移除它（在快照的資產明細中按右鍵，選擇移除）。
  2. 當資產已無任何快照值後，就可以從資產列表中刪除。

Note

資產通常透過 CSV 匯入或在快照中新增來建立。你不需要單獨建立資產，它們會在你第一次將其加入快照時自動建立。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 快照：管理快照與編輯資產值
  • 類別：將資產分組
  • 平台：管理持有資產的平台
  • 匯入 CSV：從試算表批次匯入資產與數值

docs.title: 批次輸入
docs.text: 批次輸入是一個全螢幕的工作流程，讓你在單一畫面中輸入所有平台的資產值。這是建立新快照的預設方式，設計目的是讓每月的投資組合更新更快速有效率。

docs.title: 開啟批次輸入
docs.text:
1. 在側邊欄中前往投資組合 > 快照。
  2. 點選工具列的 + 按鈕或按 Cmd+N。
  3. 選擇快照的日期。
  4. 選擇批次輸入作為建立模式（這是預設選項）。
  5. 點選建立。

批次輸入檢視會開啟，顯示最近一筆快照的所有資產，依平台分組，準備讓你更新數值。

Tip

如果這是你的第一筆快照，畫面會是空的。點選新增平台開始新增資產。

docs.title: 批次輸入表格
docs.text:
資產依字母順序組織成可摺疊的平台區塊。每個平台標題顯示：

  • 平台名稱（未指定平台的資產顯示為「無平台」）
  • 資產數量
  • 進度指標（例如「3/5」表示 5 項資產中有 3 項已更新）
  • 新增資產按鈕，用於直接建立資產
  • 匯入 CSV 按鈕，用於單一平台的 CSV 匯入

docs.title: 欄位
docs.text: 欄位 說明 包含 勾選框，決定是否將資產納入快照。排除的資產會變淡且不會被儲存。 資產名稱 現有資產為唯讀。新建的資產可編輯，並標有綠色「NEW」標籤。 類別 顯示資產的類別。新資產可從下拉選單中選擇現有類別或建立新類別。 幣別 現有資產為唯讀。新資產提供下拉選單選擇。 前次價值 最近一筆快照中的資產值，供參考。若無前次紀錄則顯示「—」。 新值 在此輸入目前的市場價值。按 Enter 可跳到下一列。

docs.title: 列狀態
docs.text:
列以顏色標示，幫助你追蹤進度：

  • 綠色背景：數值已輸入或匯入（已更新）。
  • 無特殊標示：資產已包含但尚未輸入數值（待更新）。儲存時會以 0 作為數值。
  • 半透明（50% 不透明度）：資產已排除，不會被儲存。

docs.title: 進度工具列
docs.text:
頂部的工具列以彩色指示器即時顯示進度：

  • 綠色：已更新的資產（已輸入或匯入數值）
  • 橘色：待更新的資產（已包含但尚未輸入數值）
  • 灰色：已排除的資產

docs.title: 新增資產
docs.text:
點選平台標題中的新增資產，即可新增一列。填入：

  • 資產名稱：在同一平台內必須唯一（不區分大小寫）
  • 類別：從現有類別中選擇，或選擇「新增類別⋯」直接建立
  • 幣別：預設為你的主要幣別

新資產會標有綠色 NEW 標籤。如果改變主意，可以使用垃圾桶圖示移除新增的列。

docs.title: 新增平台
docs.text: 點選工具列中的新增平台，開啟新平台彈出視窗。輸入唯一的平台名稱後點選新增。新的平台區塊會出現，包含一列空白的資產列，可立即開始編輯。

docs.title: 單一平台 CSV 匯入
docs.text:
每個平台區塊都有一個匯入 CSV 按鈕，讓你從 CSV 檔案直接匯入數值到該平台群組。

匯入時：

  • 匹配的資產（名稱相同，不區分大小寫）會更新其新值，並標上藍色 CSV 標籤。
  • 未匹配的資產會作為新列加入，並標上藍色 CSV 標籤。

匯入後，系統會顯示結果摘要：更新了多少資產、新增了多少資產，以及因平台或幣別不匹配而跳過的列。

Note

此單一平台 CSV 匯入與完整的匯入 CSV 工具（Cmd+I）是分開的。這是一種在不離開批次輸入畫面的情況下，快速帶入單一平台數值的方式。

docs.title: 驗證
docs.text:
工具列會顯示必須在儲存前解決的驗證警告：

  • 零值資產：「N 項資產的值為 0。請排除它們或輸入非零值。」值為 0 的欄位會以紅色邊框標示。
  • 缺少名稱：「部分新資產缺少名稱。」新資產列必須有名稱才能儲存。
  • 重複名稱：「同一平台內有重複的資產名稱。」重複的列都會以紅色邊框標示。

儲存快照按鈕在所有驗證問題解決前會保持停用狀態。

docs.title: 儲存快照
docs.text:
一切就緒後，點選儲存快照。如果有任何已包含的資產仍未輸入數值（待更新），系統會顯示確認對話框，詢問是否要以 0 作為數值儲存。你之後隨時可以在快照詳細檢視中更新這些數值。

儲存後，畫面會跳轉到快照詳細檢視，你可以在此檢視結果、新增現金流或進行進一步編輯。

docs.title: 零值警告
docs.text:
以 0 作為數值儲存的資產會在整個應用程式中被標記，提醒你更新它們：

  • 快照詳細檢視：每個零值資產旁會出現黃色警告圖示。將滑鼠移上去可查看詳細資訊。
  • 快照列表：包含零值資產的快照會顯示黃色警告圖示。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 快照：快照概覽與快照詳細檢視
  • 匯入 CSV：完整的 CSV 匯入工具，用於批次匯入資產與現金流
  • 資產：管理個別資產
  • 平台：管理平台

docs.title: 現金流
docs.text: 現金流記錄的是外部資金的進出，也就是存入與提領投資組合的金額。準確記錄這些資料非常重要，因為它們直接影響 AssetFlow 計算投資報酬率的方式。

docs.title: 什麼算是現金流？
docs.text:
• 存入：從外部加入投資組合的資金。例如，每月的儲蓄投入或你決定用來投資的獎金。
  • 提領：從投資組合中取出的資金。例如，賣出投資來支付某項開銷。
  • 不算現金流：在投資組合內部的資產之間移動資金（例如賣出股票來買債券）。這些內部轉移不會改變投資組合的外部總資金量。

docs.title: 新增現金流
docs.text:
1. 前往某筆快照的詳細檢視。
  2. 向下捲動到現金流操作區段。
  3. 點選新增現金流。
  4. 輸入說明與金額，存入使用正數，提領使用負數。

你也可以從 CSV 檔案批次匯入現金流。詳情請參閱匯入 CSV。

docs.title: 編輯與刪除
docs.text:
• 在任一現金流項目上按右鍵可開啟快捷選單。
  • 選擇編輯可開啟浮動視窗，修改說明與金額。
  • 選擇移除可刪除該筆現金流。

docs.title: 現金流如何影響報酬率
docs.text:
如果沒有記錄現金流，存入 $10,000 看起來會像是你的投資成長了 $10,000，但那筆錢其實是來自你的銀行帳戶，而非投資收益。AssetFlow 使用你的現金流資料來區分實際投資績效與外部資金進出，讓你準確了解投資的真實表現。

前往績效指標查看報酬率計算方式的詳細說明。

Tip

請記錄每個快照期間的所有存入與提領。現金流資料越準確，你的績效指標就越有意義。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 績效指標：了解現金流如何影響報酬率計算
  • 快照：建立與管理投資組合快照
  • 匯入 CSV：從試算表批次匯入現金流

docs.title: 類別
docs.text: 類別讓你依類型將資產分組，例如股票、債券、不動產、現金，或任何適合你投資組合的分類方式。每個類別可以設定目標配置，協助你進行再平衡。

docs.title: 類別列表
docs.text:
類別檢視的左側列出所有類別。你可以拖曳排序，這裡設定的順序會套用到整個應用程式。

每一列顯示：

  • 類別名稱
  • 目標配置 %
  • 目前配置 %
  • 目前價值
  • 資產數量
  • 偏差指示器（警告圖示），當目前配置與目標的差距超過 5% 時會出現

docs.title: 橫幅
docs.text:
列表頂部可能出現兩個資訊橫幅：

  • 目標配置加總警告：當你的目標配置總和超過 100%，或部分類別缺少目標設定時顯示。幫助你發現設定上的問題。
  • 顯著偏差提示：當一個或多個類別與目標有明顯偏差時顯示，並附上前往再平衡工具的連結。

在類別上按右鍵可刪除（僅當沒有資產被指定到該類別時才可刪除）。

docs.title: 建立類別
docs.text:
點選工具列的 + 按鈕建立新類別。你需要輸入：

  • 類別名稱：為該群組取一個描述性的名稱（例如「股票」、「固定收益」、「另類投資」）。
  • 目標配置 %：選填，介於 0 到 100 之間。這是你希望此類別在投資組合中佔的百分比。

docs.title: 類別詳細檢視
docs.text: 選取一個類別，即可在右側查看其詳細資訊。

docs.title: 屬性
docs.text:
• 名稱：可直接在詳細檢視中編輯。
  • 目標配置 %：可編輯，範圍同樣是 0 到 100。

docs.title: 類別中的資產
docs.text: 一個表格列出所有歸屬於此類別的資產，包含每項資產的名稱與平台。

docs.title: 價值歷史
docs.text: 一張折線圖顯示此類別的總值隨時間的變化。使用時間範圍選擇器可聚焦特定期間。

docs.title: 配置歷史
docs.text: 一張折線圖顯示此類別的配置百分比隨時間的變化。圖上會疊加一條目標線供你參考，讓你一眼看出實際配置與目標的差距。

docs.title: 刪除類別
docs.text:
類別只有在沒有任何資產被指定到它時才能刪除。如果類別中仍有資產，你需要先將那些資產重新指定到其他類別，然後才能刪除這個空類別。

Tip

使用再平衡工具可以一目了然地看出哪些類別超過或低於目標配置。它是你決定下一步投資方向的最快方式。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 再平衡：比較目前配置與目標
  • 資產：管理個別投資標的
  • 儀表板：在圓餅圖中查看類別配置

docs.title: 儀表板
docs.text: 儀表板是你開啟 AssetFlow 時看到的第一個畫面，也是你的主畫面（位於側邊欄的總覽 > 儀表板）。它讓你一眼掌握整個投資組合的概況。

docs.title: 摘要卡片
docs.text:
儀表板頂部有一組摘要卡片：

  • 投資組合總值：最顯眼的主要卡片，顯示你的投資組合目前的總價值。
  • 最新快照日期：你最近一筆快照的日期。
  • 資產數量：你的投資組合中有多少項資產。
  • 累計 TWR：你的歷史時間加權報酬率（業界衡量投資績效的標準方法）。
  • CAGR：你的年化複合成長率，方便你進行逐年比較。

docs.title: 期間績效卡片
docs.text:
摘要下方，你可以選擇 1M、3M 或 1Y 等時間區間，查看該期間的投資組合表現。每張績效卡片顯示：

  • 成長率：投資組合總值的簡單百分比變化。這個數字包含了存入與提領的影響。
  • 報酬率：使用修正 Dietz 方法計算的現金流調整後報酬率。這個指標排除了資金進出的影響，呈現你真正的投資表現。
  • 日期範圍副標題，標示涵蓋的確切期間。

Tip

不確定成長率與報酬率有什麼差別嗎？前往績效指標頁面查看白話說明。

docs.title: 圖表
docs.text:
儀表板包含多個互動式圖表，幫助你視覺化投資組合：

  • 類別配置圓餅圖：檢視你的投資組合在各類別間的分布。點選某個類別的扇形區域，可直接跳至該類別的詳細頁面。
  • 投資組合價值折線圖：追蹤你的投資組合總值隨時間的變化。點選任一資料點可跳至對應的快照。
  • 累計 TWR 折線圖：顯示你的時間加權報酬率隨時間的走勢，讓你了解投資績效的趨勢。
  • 類別價值折線圖：追蹤每個類別的價值隨時間的變化，方便你找出投資組合中成長最快的部分。

所有圖表都支援滑鼠懸停提示，只要將游標移到任一資料點上，就能看到確切的日期與數值。

docs.title: 最近快照
docs.text: 儀表板底部列出你最近的幾筆快照。每一列顯示快照日期、總值與資產數量。點選任一列可直接跳至該快照的詳細檢視。

docs.title: 空白狀態
docs.text: 如果你尚未建立任何快照，儀表板會顯示引導提示，幫助你開始使用。你可以選擇匯入 CSV 檔案或手動建立第一筆快照。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 績效指標：了解每個指標的意義
  • 快照：學習如何建立與管理快照
  • 類別：依類型組織你的資產

docs.title: 匯入 CSV
docs.text: AssetFlow 的 CSV 匯入功能讓你從試算表匯出的檔案中批次匯入資產與現金流。這是利用現有投資組合資料快速上手的最佳方式，也方便你批次更新持倉。

docs.title: 開啟匯入工具
docs.text:
在側邊欄中前往工具 > 匯入 CSV，或按 Cmd+I。

在匯入檢視的頂部，切換資產或現金流來選擇你要匯入的類型。

docs.title: 步驟 1：選取檔案
docs.text:
將 CSV 檔案拖放到匯入區域，或點選瀏覽從 Finder 中選取。

你的檔案至少應包含以下欄位：

欄位 必填 說明 Asset Name 是 資產名稱 Market Value 是 目前的市場價值 Platform 否 持有此資產的平台 Currency 否 資產的幣別代碼（例如 USD、TWD）

docs.title: 步驟 2：設定
docs.text:
匯入前，你可以微調資料的匯入方式：

  • 快照日期：選擇此快照代表的日期。
  • 平台：可選擇指定一個平台。如有需要，可以直接建立新平台。勾選套用至所有列會將其套用到每一項匯入的資產；不勾選則只會填入 CSV 中沒有平台資訊的列。
  • 類別：可選擇指定一個類別。同樣可以直接建立新類別。套用至所有列的運作方式相同，勾選會覆蓋所有列，不勾選只填入空白的列。
  • 複製帶入：開啟此選項可帶入不在此 CSV 中的其他平台資產。這在你只從某一家券商匯入，但想保留其他平台資產在同一筆快照中時很有用。
    • 選擇要從哪些平台複製帶入。
    • 每個選項會顯示資產數量與來源快照日期。

docs.title: 步驟 3：檢視與匯入
docs.text:
預覽表格顯示所有解析後的列及其驗證狀態：

  • 錯誤（紅色）：這些問題會阻止匯入，必須修正。常見原因包括缺少必填欄位、重複的資產，或無法解析的值。
  • 警告（黃色）：允許匯入但標記潛在問題，例如數值為零或無法辨識的幣別代碼。

你可以在匯入前直接從預覽中移除有問題的列。

確認一切無誤後，點選匯入。

Warning

如果所選日期已有快照存在，匯入會更新該快照，而非建立新的快照。現有快照中值為 0 的資產會被匯入的數值覆蓋。

docs.title: 匯入現金流
docs.text:
現金流匯入的流程與資產類似。你的 CSV 應包含：

欄位 必填 說明 Description 是 現金流的標籤（例如「每月定期投入」） Amount 是 正數代表存入，負數代表提領 Currency 否 幣別代碼

現金流會匯入你在設定時選擇的特定快照中。

Tip

你也可以直接從批次輸入檢視中針對個別平台匯入 CSV 檔案。當你只想在建立快照的過程中更新單一平台的數值時非常方便。詳情請參閱批次輸入。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• CSV 格式參考：詳細的欄位規格與範例檔案
  • 快照：了解快照的運作方式
  • 批次輸入：在單一畫面中輸入所有資產的值，支援單一平台 CSV 匯入
  • 現金流：了解更多關於追蹤存入與提領的資訊

docs.title: 績效指標
docs.text: AssetFlow 計算多項績效指標，幫助你了解投資表現。如果你曾好奇儀表板上那些數字代表什麼意思，這個頁面會用白話逐一說明。

docs.title: 成長率
docs.text:
是什麼： 投資組合總值在兩個日期之間的簡單百分比變化。

計算方式： 用期末值減去期初值，除以期初值，再乘以 100%，就是你的成長率。

重要須知： 成長率包含了存入與提領的影響。如果你在期間內存入了 $10,000，即使投資完全沒有漲跌，投資組合的成長率仍會反映這筆存入。

最適用場景： 快速了解你的總財富變化。當你想看到全貌（包含你存入或提領的資金）時很有用。

docs.title: 報酬率（修正 Dietz）
docs.text:
是什麼： 經過現金流調整的報酬率，排除了存入與提領的影響，呈現純粹的投資表現。

計算方式： 修正 Dietz 方法會考慮資金在期間中何時被存入或提領，依據實際投入的時間加權計算。月初存入的資金權重高於月底存入的資金。

為什麼重要： 如果你在月中存入 $10,000，修正 Dietz 方法不會將這筆存入計為「成長」。它只衡量你原有的資金（以及新存入的資金，從存入時間起算）實際上表現如何。

最適用場景： 了解你在單一期間的實際投資表現。當你想知道「我的投資這個月表現如何？」而不被存入或提領的金額所誤導時，看這個數字就對了。

docs.title: 累計時間加權報酬率（TWR）
docs.text:
是什麼： 將每個快照期間的報酬率串聯起來，呈現你的整體投資績效，完全消除現金流時間點的影響。

計算方式： AssetFlow 計算每兩個相鄰快照之間的報酬率（使用修正 Dietz 方法），然後將它們複合計算，就像把每個期間的成長因子相乘。最後的結果呈現的是，假設從未有過現金流進出時的整體投資表現。

為什麼重要： 即使你在市場下跌前剛好存入一大筆資金，TWR 衡量的是你的投資選擇表現如何，而非你存入資金的時間點。兩個持有相同投資但存款時間不同的人，會看到相同的 TWR。

最適用場景： 長期績效追蹤。這是業界評估投資績效的標準方法，也是基金經理人和理財顧問使用的指標。

docs.title: CAGR（年化複合成長率）
docs.text:
是什麼： 累計 TWR 的年化版本，它告訴你如果成長完全平穩一致，你的投資組合每年成長了多少。

最適用場景： 將你的投資組合績效與基準指標進行比較。當有人說「S&P 500 每年報酬 10%」時，那就是 CAGR，現在你可以用同樣的標準來比較自己的投資組合。

Tip

如果只看一個數字，建議看累計 TWR，它是最公平地衡量你的投資實際表現的指標。

Note

為了讓這些指標準確，請務必在每筆快照中記錄你的現金流（存入與提領）。沒有現金流資料，AssetFlow 就無法區分投資收益與你存入的資金。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 儀表板：實際查看這些指標
  • 現金流：了解如何記錄存入與提領

docs.title: 平台
docs.text:
平台代表你的資產存放在哪裡，例如券商帳戶、交易所、銀行或其他保管機構。它幫助你追蹤哪些資產在哪個平台上，讓你隨時知道你的資金在哪裡。

大多數時候你不需要手動建立平台。平台通常在你匯入 CSV 檔案或為資產指定平台時自動建立。

docs.title: 平台列表
docs.text:
平台檢視的左側列出所有平台，可排序顯示。

  • 拖曳排序：依你喜歡的方式排列平台順序。
  • 每一列顯示平台名稱、總值與資產數量。
  • 在任一平台上按右鍵可重新命名。

docs.title: 平台詳細檢視
docs.text:
從列表中選取一個平台，即可在右側查看其詳細資訊。

  • 名稱：顯示在頂部。在列表中的平台上按右鍵可重新命名。
  • 平台上的資產：一個表格列出此平台持有的所有資產，以及每項資產的名稱與類別。
  • 價值歷史：一張折線圖顯示此平台的總值隨時間的變化。使用時間範圍選擇器可聚焦特定期間，將游標懸停在任一資料點上可查看確切的日期與數值。

Note

你不需要手動建立平台。當你匯入 CSV 資料或為資產指定平台時，平台會自動建立。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 資產：管理個別資產並指定平台
  • 匯入 CSV：從試算表批次匯入包含平台資訊的資產

docs.title: 再平衡
docs.text:
再平衡工具讓你清楚看到目前的投資組合配置與目標的差距，並建議你需要買進或賣出多少才能回到正軌。

Note

AssetFlow 的再平衡工具是一個唯讀的計算機，僅提供建議，實際的交易需要你自行透過券商執行。

docs.title: 運作方式
docs.text: 再平衡工具將你的目前配置（根據最新快照）與你在各類別上設定的目標配置進行比較。它會計算差距，並告訴你每個類別需要買進或賣出多少才能達到目標。

docs.title: 解讀再平衡表格
docs.text:
頂部會顯示你的投資組合總值。

下方的表格分為三個區段：

  1. 有目標的類別：主要的再平衡檢視，顯示已設定目標配置的類別。
  2. 未設定目標：存在但尚未設定目標配置的類別。
  3. 未分類：尚未指定到任何類別的資產。

表格中每一列包含：

欄位 說明 類別 類別名稱 目前值 此類別中資產的總值 目前 % 此類別佔投資組合的比例 目標 % 你為此類別設定的理想配置 差距 目前配置與目標的落差 操作 達到目標所需採取的行動

操作以顏色標示，方便快速瀏覽：

  • 買進（綠色）：你需要買進的金額，才能達到目標。
  • 賣出（紅色）：你需要賣出的金額，才能降到目標。
  • 無需操作（灰色）：你已經在目標上了，做得好！

docs.title: 建議操作
docs.text: 檢視底部有一個建議操作摘要，以文字描述說明讓投資組合恢復平衡所需的主要操作。

docs.title: 前置條件
docs.text:
要使用再平衡工具，你需要：

  • 至少一筆包含資產的快照。
  • 已設定目標配置的類別。
  • 已將資產指定到類別。

Tip

先為你的類別設定目標配置（例如：股票 60%、債券 30%、現金 10%），再平衡工具就會自動幫你計算需要做什麼。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 類別：建立類別並設定目標配置
  • 資產：將資產指定到類別
  • 儀表板：綜覽你的配置分布

docs.title: 快照
docs.text: 快照是 AssetFlow 的核心概念。每筆快照記錄你的整個投資組合在某個特定時間點的狀態，就像是你的財務照片。透過比較不同時間的快照，AssetFlow 可以計算你的績效、追蹤配置變化，並呈現投資組合的演變過程。

docs.title: 快照列表
docs.text:
快照檢視的左側列出所有快照，依時間分組：本月、前 3 個月、前 6 個月、前 1 年以及更早。

每一列顯示：

  • 快照日期
  • 平台標籤（最多顯示 3 個，超過的以 +N 表示）
  • 投資組合總值
  • 資產數量

在任一快照上按右鍵，可從快捷選單中選擇刪除。

docs.title: 建立新快照
docs.text:
點選工具列的 + 按鈕或按 Cmd+N 建立新快照。

系統會要求你：

  1. 選擇日期：快照日期不可為未來日期，且同一天不能有兩筆快照。如果所選日期已有快照存在，系統會顯示警告，且建立按鈕會被停用。
  2. 選擇建立模式：
    • 批次輸入（預設）：開啟全螢幕檢視，讓你一次輸入所有資產的值，依平台分組。前次的數值會帶入供參考。這是每月更新的推薦工作流程。
    • 空白快照：建立一筆空白快照，並帶你到快照詳細檢視，可逐一新增資產。

Tip

每月更新時使用批次輸入，所有資產會從最近的快照預先載入，你只需要更新有變動的數值。詳細操作請參閱批次輸入。

docs.title: 快照詳細檢視
docs.text: 右側顯示所選快照的詳細檢視。

docs.title: 摘要
docs.text:
頂部會顯示：

  • 總值：此快照中所有資產的加總。如果快照包含值為 0 的資產，會顯示黃色警告圖示
  • 淨現金流：存入減去提領的合計，並顯示操作筆數
  • 匯率狀態：是否已取得此快照日期的匯率

docs.title: 資產明細
docs.text:
一個表格列出快照中的每項資產及其市場價值。多幣別資產會同時顯示原始幣別值與換算後的值。

  • 在資產上按右鍵可以編輯其值或從快照中移除。
  • 新增資產：點選可將現有資產加入此快照，或直接建立新資產。

docs.title: 類別配置
docs.text: 顯示此快照中涵蓋的所有類別，以及各類別的價值與佔投資組合的百分比。

docs.title: 匯率
docs.text: 顯示用於幣別轉換的匯率。建立快照時會自動取得匯率。如果取得失敗，你會看到一個重試按鈕。

docs.title: 現金流操作
docs.text: 列出此快照記錄的所有存入與提領。在任一項目上按右鍵可編輯或移除。使用新增現金流按鈕來記錄新的存入或提領。

docs.title: 編輯資產值
docs.text:
在快照中編輯資產值有兩種方式：

  1. 在資產明細中按右鍵，選擇編輯價值。
  2. 直接雙擊數值。

會彈出一個浮動視窗，讓你輸入新的市場價值。

docs.title: 刪除快照
docs.text:
若要刪除快照，請捲動到快照詳細檢視底部的危險區域，點選刪除快照。系統會顯示確認對話框，標示快照日期、資產數量與現金流筆數，讓你清楚知道將會移除哪些內容。

Warning

刪除快照會永久移除該日期的所有記錄值與現金流。資產本身不會被刪除，它們仍然存在，且會出現在其他快照中。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 批次輸入：在單一畫面中輸入所有資產的值
  • 現金流：為每筆快照記錄存入與提領
  • 資產：管理你的個別投資標的
  • 匯入 CSV：從試算表批次匯入快照資料

docs.title: CSV 格式
docs.text: AssetFlow 匯入工具接受的 CSV 檔案格式參考。

docs.title: 必填欄位
docs.text: 欄位 說明 Asset Name 資產名稱（例如「AAPL」、「Vanguard Total Stock Market ETF」） Market Value 目前的市場價值，以數字表示（例如 15000、1234.56）

docs.title: 選填欄位
docs.text: 欄位 說明 Platform 持有此資產的平台或券商 Currency 三碼幣別代碼（例如 USD、EUR、TWD）。省略時預設為你的主要幣別。

docs.title: 範例
docs.text:
Asset Name,Market Value,Platform,Currency
AAPL,15000,Interactive Brokers,USD
VTI,28000,Interactive Brokers,USD
Bitcoin,5000,Coinbase,USD
台積電,120000,永豐金證券,TWD

docs.title: 必填欄位
docs.text: 欄位 說明 Description 現金流的標籤（例如「每月定期投入」、「股息再投入」） Amount 金額，以數字表示。正數代表存入，負數代表提領。

docs.title: 選填欄位
docs.text: 欄位 說明 Currency 三碼幣別代碼。省略時預設為你的主要幣別。

docs.title: 範例
docs.text:
Description,Amount,Currency
Monthly savings,5000,USD
Emergency withdrawal,-2000,USD
年終獎金投入,100000,TWD

docs.title: 常見問題
docs.text:
問題 原因 解決方法 「缺少必填欄位」 CSV 沒有 Asset Name 或 Market Value 標題 檢查欄位標題是否完全一致 「重複的資產」 同一個資產名稱 + 平台的組合出現了兩次 移除重複的列或變更平台 「無法解析的數值」 Market Value 包含非數字字元 移除貨幣符號、數字中的逗號 「不支援的幣別」 幣別代碼無法辨識 使用標準 ISO 4217 代碼（例如 USD、EUR、TWD）

Tip

將試算表匯出為 UTF-8 CSV，以確保特殊字元（例如中文）能被正確處理。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 匯入 CSV：逐步匯入資料指南
  • 幣別與匯率：支援的幣別與匯率運作方式

docs.title: 幣別與匯率
docs.text: AssetFlow 支援多種幣別，並自動取得匯率，將所有數值換算為你的主要顯示幣別。

docs.title: 運作方式
docs.text:
• 每項資產可以用自己的幣別追蹤（例如美股用 USD，台股用 TWD）。
  • 系統會自動取得每筆快照日期的匯率。
  • 所有數值會換算為你的主要幣別顯示。你可以在設定（Cmd+,）> 偏好設定中變更主要幣別。
  • 資料來源：匯率從 cdn.jsdelivr.net 取得，提供開放原始碼的匯率資料。

docs.title: 支援的幣別
docs.text: AssetFlow 內建一組常用幣別清單。首次啟動時（以及之後定期更新），應用程式會從匯率 API 取得擴充的幣別清單，並快取在本機供離線使用。

docs.title: 內建幣別
docs.text:
以下幣別即使沒有網路連線也隨時可用：

代碼 幣別 代碼 幣別 AED 阿聯酋迪拉姆 KRW 韓元 AUD 澳幣 MXN 墨西哥披索 BRL 巴西雷亞爾 MYR 馬來西亞令吉 BTC 比特幣 NOK 挪威克朗 CAD 加幣 NZD 紐幣 CHF 瑞士法郎 PHP 菲律賓披索 CNY 人民幣 PLN 波蘭茲羅提 DKK 丹麥克朗 SAR 沙烏地里亞爾 ETH 以太幣 SEK 瑞典克朗 EUR 歐元 SGD 新加坡幣 GBP 英鎊 THB 泰銖 HKD 港幣 TRY 土耳其里拉 IDR 印尼盾 TWD 新台幣 ILS 以色列謝克爾 USD 美元 INR 印度盧比 VND 越南盾 JPY 日圓 ZAR 南非蘭特

docs.title: 完整幣別清單
docs.text:
首次成功連線後，AssetFlow 會從匯率 API 取得完整的可用幣別清單，並快取在本機。這包含了數百種法定貨幣與加密貨幣，遠超上方的內建清單。

你可以在瀏覽器中開啟 API 端點查看完整的支援幣別代碼清單。

docs.title: 自動取得行為
docs.text:
匯率在以下幾種情況下會自動取得：

  • 建立新快照時：當你建立的快照包含多種幣別的資產時，該日期的匯率會立即取得。
  • 啟動應用程式時：任何現有快照缺少的匯率會在背景中取得。
  • 還原備份後：從備份還原時，系統會重新取得所有快照的匯率。

docs.title: 無法取得匯率時
docs.text:
如果你處於離線狀態或遇到網路問題：

  • 快照詳細檢視中會顯示重試按鈕，讓你稍後再試。
  • 數值會以原始幣別顯示，不進行換算。
  • 應用程式的其他功能完全可以離線使用，匯率是唯一需要網路的功能。

Note

AssetFlow 會將取得的匯率儲存在本機。一旦某筆快照的匯率已成功取得，即使離線也不需要重新取得。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 偏好設定：設定你的主要顯示幣別
  • 資產：管理資產及其幣別
  • CSV 格式：在 CSV 匯入中指定幣別

docs.title: 鍵盤快捷鍵
docs.text:
AssetFlow 鍵盤快捷鍵速查表。

快捷鍵 功能 Cmd+N 建立新快照 Cmd+I 開啟 CSV 匯入 Cmd+, 開啟設定 Enter 跳到下一列的新值欄位（在批次輸入中使用）

Note

未來的更新可能會新增更多鍵盤快捷鍵。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 快照：了解如何建立與管理快照
  • 批次輸入：在單一畫面中輸入所有資產的值
  • 匯入 CSV：從試算表批次匯入資料

docs.title: 備份與還原
docs.text: 定期建立備份，保護你的投資組合資料。AssetFlow 會將所有資料匯出為一個 ZIP 檔案，可以隨時還原。

docs.title: 建立備份
docs.text:
1. 開啟設定（Cmd+,），前往資料管理區段。
  2. 點選匯出備份。
  3. 在儲存對話框中選擇儲存位置與檔案名稱。
  4. 系統會建立一個包含所有投資組合資料的 ZIP 檔案。

docs.title: 備份包含什麼
docs.text:
備份檔案包含你投資組合中的所有內容：

  • 所有快照及其資產值
  • 所有資產、類別與平台
  • 所有現金流操作
  • 匯率資料
  • 應用程式設定

docs.title: 從備份還原
docs.text:
1. 開啟設定（Cmd+,），前往資料管理區段。
  2. 點選從備份還原。
  3. 選取一個備份 ZIP 檔案。
  4. 確認還原。注意：這會取代所有目前的資料。
  5. 還原後，系統會自動重新取得所有快照的匯率。

Warning

從備份還原會取代所有目前的資料。此操作無法復原。如果你想保留目前的資料，請務必先匯出備份。

Tip

定期建立備份，尤其是在對投資組合資料進行重大變更之前。將備份存放在安全的位置，例如外接硬碟或雲端儲存空間。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 偏好設定：自訂顯示與匯入設定
  • 安全性：使用應用程式鎖定與驗證保護你的資料

docs.title: 偏好設定
docs.text: 自訂 AssetFlow 顯示與處理資料的方式。從選單列的 AssetFlow > 設定 開啟設定，或按 Cmd+,。

docs.title: 主要幣別
docs.text:
選擇用來顯示所有投資組合數值的幣別。當你的投資組合包含不同幣別的資產時，AssetFlow 會使用取得的匯率將所有數值換算為此主要幣別。

從下拉選單中選擇你偏好的幣別。支援的完整幣別清單請參閱幣別與匯率。

docs.title: 日期格式
docs.text: 選擇整個應用程式中日期的顯示方式。提供多種格式選項，並附有即時預覽，讓你確切看到日期會如何呈現，方便你挑選最習慣的風格。

docs.title: 匯入預設
docs.text: 設定在 CSV 匯入時預先填入的預設平台名稱。如果你經常從同一家券商或平台匯入，這可以省去你每次都要重新輸入相同名稱的麻煩。

docs.title: 關於
docs.text:
關於區段顯示：

  • 應用程式版本與建置編號
  • 開發者資訊與授權條款
  • 隱私資訊：
    • 所有資料都儲存在你的 Mac 本機
    • 匯率從 cdn.jsdelivr.net 取得
    • 不會收集或傳輸任何個人資料
  • 原始碼：專案 GitHub 儲存庫的連結

Tip

切換主要幣別後，所有投資組合的數值與圖表會立即更新為新的幣別。換算時使用的是匯率資料，因此請確保你的快照已成功取得匯率。

docs.title: 另請參閱
docs.text:
• 安全性：使用應用程式鎖定與驗證保護你的資料
  • 備份與還原：匯出與匯入你的投資組合資料
  • 幣別與匯率：多幣別支援的運作方式

docs.title: 安全性
docs.text: AssetFlow 提供選擇性的應用程式鎖定功能，保護你的財務資料不被未經授權的人存取。啟用後，一個全螢幕的遮罩會隱藏所有投資組合資料，直到你完成驗證。

docs.title: 啟用應用程式鎖定
docs.text:
1. 開啟設定（Cmd+,），前往安全性區段。
  2. 開啟需要驗證。

docs.title: 驗證方式
docs.text:
可用的驗證方式取決於你的 Mac 的功能：

  • Touch ID：適用於配備 Touch ID 感應器的 MacBook（MacBook Pro/Air 含 Touch Bar 或更新款式）
  • Apple Watch：適用於已配對 Apple Watch 的情況
  • 系統密碼：在任何 Mac 上都可作為備用方式使用

docs.title: 逾時設定
docs.text: 透過兩個獨立的逾時設定控制 AssetFlow 何時自動鎖定：

docs.title: 切換應用程式時
docs.text:
切換到其他應用程式後（例如按 Cmd+Tab），多快自動鎖定。

選項 行為 立即 離開 AssetFlow 的瞬間就鎖定 1 分鐘 離開 1 分鐘後鎖定 5 分鐘 離開 5 分鐘後鎖定 永不 維持解鎖狀態，直到 Mac 進入睡眠或螢幕鎖定

docs.title: 螢幕鎖定或睡眠時
docs.text:
Mac 螢幕鎖定或進入睡眠後，多快自動鎖定。

選項 行為 立即 螢幕鎖定或睡眠的瞬間就鎖定 1 分鐘 螢幕鎖定或睡眠 1 分鐘後鎖定 5 分鐘 螢幕鎖定或睡眠 5 分鐘後鎖定 永不 即使經過睡眠週期仍維持解鎖狀態

Tip

如果你經常在 AssetFlow 和試算表之間切換更新數值，可以將「切換應用程式時」設為「永不」。

docs.title: 鎖定畫面
docs.text:
鎖定時，一個全螢幕的遮罩會完全隱藏所有投資組合資料。若要解鎖：

  1. 在鎖定畫面上點選解鎖。
  2. 使用 Touch ID、Apple Watch 或系統密碼進行驗證。

應用程式在變為使用中狀態時會自動嘗試解鎖，所以在很多情況下，你只需要看一眼 Touch ID 感應器或輕觸 Apple Watch 即可。

Note

當應用程式處於鎖定狀態時，工具提示與滑鼠懸停效果也會被隱藏，以防止資料透過鎖定遮罩洩漏。

docs.text:
